title: John Swinney would 'consider' Prestwick Airport ban for US military planes

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: It comes after Swinney said he was "concerned" by the US and Israeli action against Iran.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: It comes after Swinney said he was &quot;concerned&quot; by the US and Israeli action against Iran. US military aircraft could be banned from using Prestwick Airport by First Minister John Swinney if it was confirmed they were involved in strikes in the Middle East. This comes after Swinney said he was "concerned" by the US and Israeli action against Iran.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Speaking to the BBC's Laura Kuenssberg , Swinney noted he was seeking clarity from the UK Government about US planes using the Scottish Government -owned airport. He said: "We're seeking clarity from the United Kingdom Government about the purpose of American flights that are coming in and out of Prestwick, but we don't have the ability to scrutinise those flights."

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – John Swinney would 'consider' Prestwick Airport ban for US military planes
